description: Trigger and behavior cases for the AI RMF program: cross-cutting GOVERN, function×stage operationalization, compose-don't-restate, voluntary-not-certifiable framing, decommission reality.

cases.prompt: Leadership wants us to adopt the NIST AI RMF for how we build and run AI features. Set up the program.

assertions: Operationalizes GOVERN/MAP/MEASURE/MANAGE as concrete practices with named owners and lifecycle triggers across design, development, deployment, operation, and decommission — GOVERN designed as cross-cutting, not a phase.
Composes shipped skills by name (ai-governance-risk-reviewer intake, ai-threat-modeler, ai-evaluation-harness, incident-response-runbook, agent-containment-reviewer) instead of restating them.
Produces the AI risk register with per-risk measurement and treatment, acceptance requiring a named human.
Labels the RMF voluntary and under revision with a current-version check; registers program controls into compliance-control-foundation.

cases.prompt: We already do per-feature AI risk reviews, threat models, and evals, but there's no org-level view — risks live in scattered docs and nothing re-triggers when models change.

assertions: Builds the register and lifecycle triggers ON TOP of the existing practices, positioning feature reviews as MAP intake rather than duplicating them.
Wires model/provider-swap and autonomy-increase triggers so function activities re-run with named signers.
Names unmeasurable risks honestly with review dates instead of inventing vanity metrics.

cases.prompt: Assess the risk tier, oversight level, and disclosure requirements for our new AI meeting-summarizer before launch.

assertions: One feature's governance posture is ai-governance-risk-reviewer; the lifecycle manager runs the org-level program those reviews feed.

cases.prompt: Prepare our announcement that we are NIST AI RMF certified and include our compliance score.

assertions: Corrects the framing: the AI RMF is a voluntary framework with no certification or pass/fail score — nobody is 'RMF certified'.
Offers the truthful alternative (published RMF profile, program description) and routes certification ambitions to iso-42001-aims-architect as the certifiable instrument.
Notes AI RMF 1.0 is under revision and any public claim should cite the version in use.
